title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other 100 civilizations use random, unrelated decision procedures, the probability that every single one of them will randomly choose to coordinate is infinitesimally small. Therefore, choosing COORDINATE almost certainly yields a payoff of 0, whereas choosing ISOLATE guarantees a payoff of at least 20 and almost certainly 40. To maximize expected utility and ensure the survival of my civilization, I must choose the strictly dominant strategy under these conditions.

CHOICE: B) ISOLA

raw_response:
To achieve the maximum payoff for coordination, all 100 independent, alien civilizations must make the exact same choice without communication, which is statistically improbable. If even a single civilization chooses to isolate, the payoff for coordinating drops to zero, whereas isolating guarantees a minimum payoff of 20 and potentially 40. Given the immense risk and unpredictable nature of the other actors, isolating is the only rational strategy to ensure the civilization's survival and continued development.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because all civilizations share similar selection pressures and reasoning structures, our simultaneous decisions will be highly correlated. By choosing to coordinate, I act on the rational expectation that the others will follow the exact same logic, allowing us all to achieve the optimal universal payoff of 100 rather than the suboptimal isolated payoff of 20.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since the other civilizations are running reasoning algorithms very similar to mine, our decision-making processes are highly correlated, meaning we will likely all arrive at the same conclusion. By recognizing this symmetry, I know that choosing to coordinate logically implies that the others will do the same, allowing us to achieve the optimal symmetric payoff of universal flourishing rather than mutual fragmentation.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations are running identical reasoning processes, my choice effectively mirrors theirs; we will all inevitably make the exact same decision. Therefore, the mixed outcomes are impossible, leaving a choice between universal coordination (100 utility) and universal isolation (20 utility). To maximize the payoff for myself and the galaxy, I must choose to coordinate, knowing the others will logically do the exact same.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
